req.title: Update patient (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall update patient demographics; historical versions must be preserved. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-053 mandates that a clinician can update patient demographics with version preservation, and that negative paths (permission denial, missing mandatory fields, version conflict) are covered. The violation reported was that test[3] had kind=negative but heuristic=STC; STC (State Transition Coverage) is valid for negative tests when the scenario exercises an invalid state transition (stale version conflict), so the fix is to correct the heuristic to NEG which is the appropriate heuristic for error-pathway negative tests, while keeping the test logic identical. Tests: (1) positive/EP confirming valid PUT returns 200 and increments versionId; (2) negative/NEG confirming a non-clinician role receives 403; (3) negative/DT confirming missing mandatory field returns 400; (4) negative/NEG confirming stale versionId produces 409 conflict; (5) boundary/BVA sending a name field of exactly 1 character to verify acceptance.

tests.name: test_clinician_update_patient_valid_preserves_history
preconditions: A patient resource exists with a known id and versionId
Caller is authenticated as a clinician role
actions: PUT /Patient/{id} with If-Match header set to current ETag and updated family name
GET /Patient/{id}/_history to verify previous version is retained
expected: Response body versionId is incremented by 1
History endpoint returns at least 2 entries including the previous version
trace: R-HLT-053: clinician shall update patient demographics
R-HLT-053: historical versions must be preserved
tests.executable:
def test_clinician_update_patient_valid_preserves_history(clinician_client, existing_patient):
    pid = existing_patient["id"]
    old_version = existing_patient["meta"]["versionId"]
    old_etag = f'W/"{old_version}"'

    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "id": pid,
        "name": [{"family": "UpdatedFamily", "given": ["Jane"]}]
    }
    resp = clinician_client.put(
        f"/Patient/{pid}",
        json=payload,
        headers={"If-Match": old_etag, "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    new_version = body["meta"]["versionId"]
    assert int(new_version) == int(old_version) + 1

    hist = clinician_client.get(f"/Patient/{pid}/_history")
    assert hist.status_code == 200
    entries = hist.json().get("entry", [])
    version_ids = [e["resource"]["meta"]["versionId"] for e in entries]
    assert old_version in version_ids
    assert new_version in version_ids

tests.name: test_non_clinician_update_patient_returns_403
preconditions: A patient resource exists with a known id
Caller is authenticated as a guest/read-only role (not clinician)
actions: PUT /Patient/{id} with valid payload using a non-clinician token
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome indicating insufficient privileges

tests.name: test_update_patient_missing_resource_type_returns_400
preconditions: A patient resource exists with a known id
Caller is authenticated as a clinician role
actions: PUT /Patient/{id} with a payload that omits the mandatory resourceType field
expected: Response body OperationOutcome references missing mandatory element
trace: R-HLT-053: error pathway — missing mandatory elements
tests.executable:
def test_update_patient_missing_resource_type_returns_400(clinician_client, existing_patient):
    pid = existing_patient["id"]
    old_version = existing_patient["meta"]["versionId"]
    old_etag = f'W/"{old_version}"'

    payload = {
        "id": pid,
        "name": [{"family": "NoResourceType"}]
    }
    resp = clinician_client.put(
        f"/Patient/{pid}",
        json=payload,
        headers={"If-Match": old_etag, "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any("resourceType" in str(i) for i in issues)

tests.name: test_update_patient_stale_version_returns_409
preconditions: A patient resource exists with a known id and versionId V
A second update has already advanced the resource to versionId V+1
Caller is authenticated as a clinician role
actions: PUT /Patient/{id} with If-Match header referencing the outdated ETag (V, not V+1)
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome indicating version conflict
trace: R-HLT-053: error pathway — 409 on version conflict
R-HLT-053: conflicting state transitions must be tested

tests.name: test_update_patient_name_single_char_boundary_accepted
preconditions: A patient resource exists with a known id
Caller is authenticated as a clinician role
actions: PUT /Patient/{id} with family name set to exactly 1 character (lower BVA boundary)
expected: Stored family name equals the single character supplied
trace: R-HLT-053: clinician shall update patient demographics
R-HLT-053: boundary on name field length (minimum valid value)
tests.executable:
def test_update_patient_name_single_char_boundary_accepted(clinician_client, existing_patient):
    pid = existing_patient["id"]
    old_version = existing_patient["meta"]["versionId"]
    old_etag = f'W/"{old_version}"'

    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "id": pid,
        "name": [{"family": "X", "given": ["Y"]}]
    }
    resp = clinician_client.put(
        f"/Patient/{pid}",
        json=payload,
        headers={"If-Match": old_etag, "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    names = body.get("name", [])
    assert any(n.get("family") == "X" for n in names)
